etymology.description: Depicts a valley with 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') representing the enclosed area -> originally a pictorial representation of a valley or ravine
etymology.interpretation: 㕣 originally depicts a valley or ravine, with 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') symbolizing an enclosed area between mountain walls. It captures the visual impression of a narrow space or passage, similar to the concept of looking over such geographical features.
